data.title: Smartee hosts European Children's Orthodontic Summit at Disneyland Paris, bringing together 52 orthodontists from 8 countries.
data.description: In Paris on December 18, 2025, the European Children's Orthodontic Summit was held by Smartee Denti-Technology at Disneyland® Paris. The event united 52 orthodontists from various countries including France, Switzerland, Belgium, the Netherlands, Spain, Algeria, and Tunisia.

data.title: Holiday travel in the US is breaking records, with 122 million Americans expected to travel this season, a 2% increase from last year. Confidence and falling gas prices are driving the surge in travel.
data.description: This holiday season, holiday travel in the United States is set to break records yet again, with an estimated 122 million Americans expected to travel, representing a 2% increase compared to last year. The surge in travel is fueled by lower gas prices and a growing consumer confidence. Gene Boehm, President and CEO of AAA Inc., stated that the trend of surpassing records each year post-pandemic continues, with the majority of holiday travelers, 89%, opting to hit the highways. This information was disclosed during Boehm's appearance on CNBC's "Squawk Box." This increase in travel signifies a significant uptick in economic activity and consumer sentiment.

data.title: OBOOK Holdings Inc. delays first half 2025 financial results call to December 29, 2025.
data.description: OBOOK Holdings Inc. has postponed its conference call announcing the financial results for the first half of 2025. The new date for the conference call is now set for December 29, 2025. This rescheduling affects the announcement of key numbers like revenue, profits, and other financial metrics for the period. Investors and stakeholders are expected to tune in on the new date to gain insights into the company's performance during the specified time frame.

data.title: Bitcoin failed as a 'store of value' in 2025, but gold-backed cryptocurrencies like Tether Gold and PAX Gold surged over 72%.
data.description: In 2025, Bitcoin did not fulfill its "digital gold" reputation as expected, while cryptocurrencies backed by precious metals emerged as the true store of value. Gold-linked crypto derivatives such as Tether Gold (XAUT) and PAX Gold (PAXG) experienced a remarkable surge of over 72% throughout the year. These digital assets have outperformed Bitcoin during this period, indicating a growing interest in precious metal-backed cryptocurrencies as viable investment options.

data.title: LEIFRAS Co., Ltd. partners with Tachibana Gakuen Educational Corporation and Matsumoto Co., Ltd. to address school refusal.
data.description: Three organizations, Leifras Co., Ltd., Tachibana Gakuen Educational Corporation, and Matsumoto Co., Ltd., have formed a Memorandum of Understanding in Tokyo on December 26, 2025. LEIFRAS Co., Ltd., a company listed on Nasdaq under the symbol LFS, specializing in youth sports and social businesses, is part of this agreement.

data.title: Trust Wallet to reimburse $7M lost in hack; extension leaked data, possible insider involvement: SlowMist.
data.description: According to cybersecurity firm SlowMist, the harmful Trust Wallet extension has been found exporting personal data of users, suggesting possible insider involvement.

data.title: S&P 500 reaches new high, investor sentiment improves in "Greed" zone. Dow up 250+ points, NYSE closed early for Christmas.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index showed a continued improvement in overall market sentiment, staying in the "Greed" zone on Wednesday. U.S. stocks finished positively, with the Dow Jones index rising over 250 points and the S&P 500 reaching new records. The New York Stock Exchange shut down early at 1 p.m. ET on Wednesday and stayed closed for Christmas Day. Nike (NYSE: NKE) saw gains following Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) CEO Tim Cook's purchase of 50,000 shares. For more details, check out the complete article on Benzinga.com.
